subject: english
learning_objectives: Define an adjective and identify it by its suffixes
Identify adjectival phrases of different types in sentences

sections.heading: Adjective – Review
sections.content:
An adjective is a word that qualifies or modifies a noun or pronoun. Adjectives can often be identified by their suffixes:
  -able: drinkable | -ful: successful

sections.heading: Adjectival Phrase – Definition
sections.content: An adjectival phrase is a phrase that performs the work of an adjective – it qualifies or describes a noun or pronoun.

sections.heading: Types and Examples of Adjectival Phrases
sections.content:
Prepositional phrase as adjectival phrase:
  The man with the red hair is my brother. (qualifying 'man')
  He is a man of great wealth. (qualifying 'man')

Participial phrase as adjectival phrase:
  A person bringing good news is always welcome.
  The woman standing over there is our new principal.
  The boy selling garri is my brother.
  The coat made of cotton is very lovely.

Relative clause as adjectival phrase:
  I know a girl who has strange appetites. (qualifying 'girl')
  The student forgot to bring back the bag that I gave her. (qualifying 'bag')

Single adjective (predicative):
  The beautiful girl has been kidnapped. (qualifying 'girl')
